channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: StudentAid.gov website went down, following mass layoffs at Department of Education
item.description: An hourslong outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: North American Bird Populations Plummet - 112 Species in Peril
item.description: A new report reveals alarming declines in North American bird populations, with over half of species losing more than 50% of their numbers in the past 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An inside look at Las Vegas valley’s first AI-powered hotel
item.description: Otonomous Hotel, under construction at Russell and Decatur, uses machine learning to create a personalized experience for guests.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, NewsNation

item.title: Steam Spring Sale 2025: Start Time, Confirmed Games, and Predictions
item.description: Get ready for the Steam Spring Sale 2025! Find out when it starts, what games are confirmed to be on sale, and our predictions for other great deals.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: SpaceX delays flight to retrieve astronauts
item.description: NASA and SpaceX canceled Wednesday’s planned launch of the Crew-10 mission to retrieve astronauts from the International Space Station (ISS), citing an issue with the launch pad.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Try these hybrid tools for thinking on paper
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I like thinking on paper. That’s why I’ve got a box under my desk with 27 old idea notebooks.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Google unveils Gemma 3 multi-modal AI models
item.description: Google DeepMind has introduced Gemma 3, an update to the company’s family of generative AI models, featuring multi-modality that allows the models to analyze images, answer questions about images, identify objects, and perform other tasks that involve analyzing and understanding visual data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Majority of Americans have used AI models like ChatGPT: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans have used ChatGPT-like artificial intelligence (AI) models according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese cyberespionage group deploys custom backdoors on Juniper routers
item.description: A Chinese cyberespionage group with a history of exploiting proprietary network-edge devices and developing custom malware for them has also been targeting enterprise and ISP-grade Juniper MX Series routers, according to a report by Google’s Mandiant team.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Arista delivers intelligent load balancing, AI job-centric observability
item.description: Arista Networks has added load balancing and AI job-centric observability features to core software products in an effort to help enterprise customers grow and effectively manage AI networked environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NASA’s new AI satellites could revolutionize disaster response
item.description: Satellite-based disaster monitoring has been a slow and tedious process for decades. The process consists of capturing images, transmitting them back to Earth, and relying on human analysts to interpret the data.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: How the Trump administration plans to use algorithms to target protesters
item.description: As protests against the Trump administration and in favor of Palestine continue to grow across the country, the U.S. State Department is reportedly planning to use tech to try and tamp down on dissent.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Apple Photos could actually win you over in iOS 18.4 – here are 4 improvements that are coming
item.description: Apple's iOS 18.4 update is right around the corner and we're excited to see what new features it's going to bring - including these four new additions to the Apple Photos app.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Designing a dynamic web application with Astro
item.description: Astro.js is among the most popular meta-frameworks for JavaScript developers, and for good reason. Astro makes it relatively easy to structure web applications for maximum effect with minimal effort.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nebraska Legislature approves measure classifying gig economy workers as independent contractors
item.description: The Nebraska Legislature has approved a measure classifying some gig economy workers as independent contractors, according to its website. L.B. 229, introduced by state Sen. Bob Hallstrom, was approved Monday, with 33 voting in favor and 15 opposed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Lummis introduces bill to create bitcoin strategic reserve
item.description: Sen. Cynthia Lummis (R-Wyo.) reintroduced legislation Tuesday to create a bitcoin strategic reserve, seeking to codify President Trump’s executive order last week establishing a reserve.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence
item.description: The U.S. Copyright Office completed the second of two studies of AI and copyrights as it dealt with whether you can copyright outputs created using AI. The post The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Observe links end-user experience with back-end troubleshooting
item.description: Observe has bolstered its observability platform with frontend monitoring capabilities that it says will enable developers and IT teams to gain visibility into application performance on end-user browsers and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk having ‘great difficulty’ running businesses amid DOGE obligations, Tesla stock slide
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk admitted he is having “great difficulty” running his various businesses as he juggles an advisory role in the Trump administration and leads the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) efforts to overhaul the federal workforce, all while Tesla stocks fall.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 3 of the best LLM integration tools for R
item.description: When we first looked at this space in late 2023, many generative AI R packages focused largely on OpenAI and ChatGPT-like functionality or coding add-ins. Today, the landscape includes packages that natively support more LLM suppliers—including models run locally on your own computer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is that iPhone app spying? Apple’s App Privacy Report reveals all
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges you to use Apple’s App Privacy Report to see what your apps are really up to.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5
